title: Ascocentrum micranthum
intro: Ascocentrum micranthum, also known as the 'Paphiopedilum-like Ascocentrum,' is an orchid species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Ascocentrum micranthum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ascocentrum micranthum is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Vandeae. The genus Ascocentrum is part of the subtribe Aeridinae. Ascocentrum micranthum is native to India and other parts of Asia, where it grows as an epiphyte in tropical forests. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the orchid family.

Its medicinal uses include treating various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. side_effects: Ascocentrum micranthum is a species of orchid, and as with any plant, it can cause some side effects in people who handle or ingest it. The sap of the Ascocentrum micranthum plant contains a chemical called calanthein, which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingesting any part of the plant is not recommended and can lead to gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting.
